# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std/http/server.ts";

const loadModule = async (modulePath: string) => {
  try {
    const module = await import(modulePath);
    return module;
  } catch (error) {
    console.error(`Failed to load module: ${modulePath}`, error);
    return null;
  }
};

const handler = async (request: Request): Promise<Response> => {
  const url = new URL(request.url);
  const modulePath = url.searchParams.get("module") || Deno.env.get("MODULE_PATH");

  if (!modulePath) {
    return new Response("Module path not specified", { status: 400 });
  }

  const module = await loadModule(modulePath);
  
  if (!module) {
    return new Response("Module loading failed", { status: 500 });
  }

  // Assume the module exports a function named `execute`
  if (typeof module.execute === "function") {
    const result = await module.execute();
    return new Response(JSON.stringify(result), { status: 200, headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" } });
  }

  return new Response("Module does not export an execute function", { status: 500 });
};

serve(handler);
